overview.creative_north_star: A Wikipedia-meets-print-catalog encyclopedia — authoritative serif typography, hard editorial borders, and card-based discovery with a scholarly tone
overview.description: Merges the informational density and blue-link conventions of a wiki reference with the bold borders, hard shadows, and card grids of a paper catalog. Headings use Libre Bodoni serif with underline borders. Navigation is uppercase tracked. Components are presented in bordered cards with hard-shadow hover effects.

palette.notes: Wikipedia blue primary. Warm near-black foreground with a slight blue tint (hue 250). All neutrals carry a faint blue cast for cohesion.

rules: Primary blue used for links, accents, active states, and ring focus
Foreground is warm near-black, never pure black
Card and muted surfaces carry a faint blue-gray tint
Borders are visible (0.72 lightness) — not subtle
Hard shadows use near-black oklch(0.13 0 0) for print-catalog effect
colors.signature_effect: hard-shadow: 4px 4px 0 oklch(0.13 0 0) — applied on hover for cards, buttons, and focus for inputs

display.usage: All headings (h1–h6), hero titles, card titles, sidebar headings. Always bold with border-bottom underline.

body.usage: Body text, descriptions, labels, navigation. Clean geometric sans for readability.

mono.usage: Code blocks, install commands, prop types

elevation.approach: Flat with hard shadows — no blur, no soft elevation. Depth created through 2px borders and offset box-shadows.
elevation.ghost_border: 2px solid foreground for cards, tabs, inputs; 1px solid border for table cells and heading underlines

buttons.primary: bg-foreground text-background border-2 border-foreground, uppercase tracking-widest text-sm font-semibold

chips.style: border-2 border-foreground px-3 py-1, uppercase tracking-widest text-xs font-semibold. Active: bg-foreground text-background. Inactive: bg-background text-foreground.

tabs.style: border-b-2 border-foreground. Active tab: bg-foreground text-background. Inactive: text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground. Content panel: border-2 border-t-0 border-foreground.

tables.style: Wikipedia-like: collapsed borders, 1px solid border, alternating row backgrounds using color-mix(in oklch, muted, transparent 50%). Header row: bg-muted font-weight-600.

sections: Hero with centered serif heading + search bar
Three-column card grid on browse (sm:2, lg:3)
Two-column detail: main content with tabs + metadata sidebar (1fr + 300px)
layout.cta_placement: Inside metadata sidebar as full-width button; inline in cards as install command preview
layout.philosophy: Information-dense but scannable. Serif headings provide editorial authority. Hard borders and shadows give physical weight. Uppercase labels create clear hierarchy.

base_styles.headings: font-serif font-bold, border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border), padding-bottom: 0.25em, margin-bottom: 0.5em
base_styles.links: color: var(--primary), text-decoration: none, hover: underline
base_styles.nav_links: text-sm font-semibold uppercase tracking-widest text-foreground hover:text-accent
base_styles.tag_pills: border border-foreground px-2 py-0.5 text-[10px] font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider

dos: Use 2px borders on cards, tabs, inputs, and header
Apply hard-shadow on hover/focus for interactive elements
Use serif (Libre Bodoni) for all headings
Use uppercase tracking-widest for labels, nav, chips, and metadata keys
Keep heading underline borders (1px solid border)
Use Wikipedia blue for links and primary actions
Use dt/dd pairs for metadata, not tables
donts: Don't use soft shadows or blur-based elevation
Don't use rounded corners larger than 0.25rem
Don't use hex colors — always OKLCH
Don't use subtle/invisible borders — borders should be prominent
Don't remove heading underlines — they're a core wiki identity element
